flashcards.back: A fault-tolerant protocol that enables all participants in a blockchain network to achieve agreement on a single data state, ensuring security and trust in a decentralized environment.

flashcards.front: What is the Byzantine Generals' Problem?
flashcards.back: A logical dilemma illustrating the challenge of achieving agreement among distributed parties when some may be unreliable or malicious, which consensus mechanisms aim to solve.

flashcards.front: What does CAP stand for in the CAP Theorem?
flashcards.back: Consistency (all nodes see the same data), Availability (network responds to requests), and Partition Tolerance (network functions despite connection failures).
Nodes (miners) compete to solve complex cryptographic puzzles requiring significant computational power. The first to solve it gets to add the next block and receives cryptocurrency as a reward.

flashcards.front: What are the main types of consensus mechanisms?
flashcards.back: The main types include Proof-of-Work (PoW), Proof-of-Stake (PoS), Delegated Proof-of-Stake (DPoS), and Practical Byzantine Fault Tolerance (PBFT), each with different approaches to achieving consensus.
